videos.title: UK | 03rd May, Wednesday - RIFT Training
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 03rd May, Wednesday - RIFT Training 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

Odysseus (PewDiePie’s Open-Source AI Agent): Local ChatGPT-Style Workspace Tested

The video tests Odysseus, PewDiePie’s free, open-source, self-hosted AI workspace that runs locally (offline) like ChatGPT or Claude but on your own computer with your data. It shows the app’s retro, somewhat messy UI and key features including chat, an agent for tasks, deep research, document writing, memories/skills, notes, tasks, calendar, email, a library for documents, scheduled task triggers, and model switching. Setup is shown via GitHub instructions (Docker or native macOS), creating an admin account, adding an API “brain,” and running on a local address. The script demonstrates using local models (including Gemma) or cloud models via OpenRouter, the “cookbook” for selecting downloadable local models, and a side-by-side model comparison, concluding that it works and is lightweight aside from local model requirements.

videos.title: Hindi | 03rd May, Wednesday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 03rd May, Wednesday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

videos.title: Jon Cooper Wins the Jack Adams Award. What's It Mean For Jared Bednar?
videos.summary_source_text:
Jon Cooper Wins the Jack Adams Award. What's It Mean For Jared Bednar? John Cooper snags the Jack Adams Award, but controversy rises as Lindy Ruff’s remarkable turnaround with the Buffalo Sabres is overlooked. Should the NHL honor overdue coaching excellence, or is the award reserved for this season’s true breakout? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson debate whether Cooper’s Tampa Bay Lightning tenure finally merited the trophy over Ruff’s miracle run and Dan Muse’s Pittsburgh Penguins surge, while pondering if Colorado Avalanche coach Jared Bednar is next in line.

The hosts break down tight voting margins, dissect Bednar’s long-term prospects, and discuss how GM shakeups could reshape award narratives. Shifting gears, they critique the NHL’s new All-Star Game format—with a five-minute, three-on-three round robin between national teams—and sound off on restricting the skills competition to players under 25. Will these changes spark fan engagement, or is it time to rethink the All-Star break altogether?

Run Gemma 4 Locally in Hermes Agent (Free AI Automation + New Web UI Setup)

The script explains how to combine Google’s newly released Gemma 4 12B model with Hermes Agent to run a free, local AI agent designed for agentic reasoning and automation. It shows examples built with Hermes Agent and an agentic operating system, including games, a Pomodoro timer, a color palette, and animations. Setup is demonstrated via Ollama (download Ollama, run the command to launch Hermes with Gemma 4) or through Hermes Agent’s new web UI to select Gemma 4 as the model. It also suggests configuring a stronger main model with Gemma 4 as a sub-agent to save tokens, and notes an alternative free API option via OpenRouter for Gemma 4 26B/31B if local hardware is limited.

Headroom: Free Open-Source Tool to Cut AI Agent Token Use by 60–95%

This episode introduces Headroom, a free open-source project trending on GitHub that compresses what AI agents read to reduce token usage by 60–95% without losing meaning. It’s described as a “zip file” for agent context—files, tools, search results, logs, and conversation history—helping agents run faster, cost less (especially via APIs), and forget less due to context-window limits. The script claims Headroom can plug into many agents and tools (including Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenClaude, and Hermes) and offers proof examples such as 92% token savings and compressing 10,144 words to 1,260 while finding the same log error. It outlines a three-step approach: crush, keep (shared reversible memory), and compound (learn from failures).

videos.title: An Easy Drill for Cleaner Chips #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
An Easy Drill for Cleaner Chips #golfswing #golftips If you struggle with inconsistent contact when chipping, this easy setup drill will change the way you strike the ball. Too many golfers lean back like they’re hitting a driver, which causes thin and heavy shots. Instead, set up normally, then slide your left hand down your right leg — this levels your shoulders and adds a touch more pressure to your lead side.
You’ll instantly feel a steeper setup and cleaner contact around the greens.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. If you struggle with inconsistent contact when chipping, this easy setup drill will change the way you strike the ball. Gemma 4 12B Just Dropped: Free Agentic Open-Source Model You Can Run Locally

Google has released Gemma 4 12B, a new free, open-source, agentic model you can access now and run locally, and the creator demonstrates building real apps inside an agent operating system by plugging Gemma 4 into Hermes Agent. Examples shown include a mouse-follow animation app, a color palette designer, a Pomodoro timer, generative art, a simple game, a website, and a wallpaper, emphasizing improved usefulness versus earlier Gemma versions. The script explains how to get Gemma 4 via Ollama (recently updated), or use free Gemma 4 API options like a 26B model via OpenRouter if you lack powerful hardware, and highlights core specs such as 16GB memory and a 256K context window along with benchmark claims around 77% reasoning and ~72% coding.

videos.title: Claude Code Worked All Night (No Human)
videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Code Worked All Night (No Human) 👑 Premium Community: https://mrc.fm/premium

Last week I showed you tank. My self hosted mission control for Claude Code that runs entirely on my own server. This week I pushed it as far as it goes. I queued up real work, walked away, and let Claude Code build on my app all night long with zero human in the loop. No babysitting, no approving every step, no me. My agents run with permissions skipped and live in persistent tmux sessions, so they just keep going while I sleep and I woke up to features actually shipped, committed, and merged into my own Forgejo repo. In this video I walk through exactly what I set up what it built overnight. If you want the full tank source to try this yourself, it's in my premium community with the coupon code tank link above. Watch till the end before you trust an AI to code unsupervised through the night.

videos.title: The Biggest Nutrition Mistake We’ve Made
videos.summary_source_text:
The Biggest Nutrition Mistake We’ve Made For decades, food has been reduced to calories, protein, carbs, and fat. But real food is far more than numbers on a nutrition label. It contains minerals, trace elements, phytonutrients, and thousands of natural compounds that work together to support human health. The goal of eating is not simply to get enough calories, it's to provide the body with the complexity and nourishment that only real food can offer.

The shift is simple: Microsoft wants to become OpenAI’s partner and its biggest AI threat at the same time.

#ai #microsoft #openai
videos.summary: Microsoft Just Shocked The Entire AI World: 7 New AI Models Microsoft just revealed seven in-house AI models, Microsoft IQ, Scout, Codename MDASH, and Majorana 2, turning its AI push into a full-stack challenge to OpenAI. MAI-Thinking-1 gives Microsoft its own reasoning model, Scout gives it an always-on agent, and Majorana 2 gives it a bigger quantum play.

videos.title: Golden Knights Rally Past Hurricanes in Game 1 | Stanley Cup Final Breakdown
videos.summary_source_text:
Golden Knights Rally Past Hurricanes in Game 1 | Stanley Cup Final Breakdown Irfaan Gaffar and David Pagnotta report live from Lenovo Center in Carolina following an entertaining Game 1 of the Stanley Cup Final as the Vegas Golden Knights rallied from an early 2-0 deficit to defeat the Carolina Hurricanes and take a 1-0 series lead. The duo breaks down how Vegas settled into the game after a rocky start, the adjustments that helped swing momentum, and why the Golden Knights were able to take control despite Carolina's strong opening period. They also discuss the Hurricanes' struggling top line, what Rod Brind'Amour's group needs to fix before Game 2, whether Carolina can afford to head back to Vegas down 2-0 in the series, and the biggest storylines emerging from the Stanley Cup Final. Plus, reaction to a wild nine-goal Game 1, key performances, goaltending, tactical adjustments, and what to watch for as the series continues.

Chapters:
0:00 Vegas Takes Game 1
0:17 Historic Stanley Cup Final Stat
0:41 Carolina's Fast Start
1:24 How Vegas Took Control
1:43 A Wild Game 1
2:08 Adjustments for Both Teams
2:45 Carolina's Top Line Concerns
3:30 Why Game 2 Is So Important
4:18 Can Carolina Respond?
4:43 Final Thoughts & Game 2 Preview

videos.title: Most Players Gas Themselves Because Their Stride Cadence Is Wrong
videos.summary_source_text:
Most Players Gas Themselves Because Their Stride Cadence Is Wrong The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

Stars Take MAJOR Step Towards Leaving Dallas For Billion-Dollar Arena In Plano | DLLS Stars Podcast Take advantage of our Playoff Diehard Sale and join our community today! https://alldlls.com/stars-playoff-offer-youtube 

The Dallas Stars sent shockwaves through the DFW Metroplex on Tuesday when the team announced they have signed a non-binding Letter of Intent and have submitted it to the City of Plano for consideration at the upcoming June 8th Plano City Council meeting. The Stars are proposing a billion-dollar sports and entertainment district at The Shops at Willow Bend mall site. This announcement came one day after the Dallas Mavericks took steps to purchase the former Valley View Mall site for their own potential new arena and entertainment district. Both teams’ current leases with the City of Dallas at American Airlines Center expire in 2031. We will also discuss last night’s Game 1 of the Stanley Cup Final, as the Vegas Golden Knights beat the Carolina Hurricanes 5-4 at Lenovo Center in Raleigh. And, if we have time, there are a lot of NHL News & Notes to discuss!

videos.title: Golden Knights Take Game 1 in Carolina ft. Brian Burke & Bill McCreary | The Sheet with Jeff Marek
videos.summary_source_text:
Golden Knights Take Game 1 in Carolina ft. Brian Burke & Bill McCreary | The Sheet with Jeff Marek The Sheet is live from Just Dishin Headquarters in Buffalo ahead of the NHL Draft Combine as Jeff Marek breaks down all the latest storylines from the Stanley Cup Final and around the NHL. Jeff is joined by Brian Burke to react to the Vegas Golden Knights taking a 1-0 series lead over the Carolina Hurricanes with a thrilling 4-3 win in Raleigh. The pair dive into what separated Vegas in Game 1, the goaltending battle between Adin Hill and Frederik Andersen, early Conn Smythe Trophy candidates, Carolina's struggling top line, key adjustments heading into Game 2, and what the Hurricanes need to do to even the series. Later, legendary NHL referee Bill McCreary joins the show to discuss his new book, share stories from his remarkable officiating career, explain the differences between calling games in the regular season versus the Stanley Cup Playoffs, and reflect on some of the biggest moments and personalities he encountered throughout his time in the NHL. From Stanley Cup Final analysis to behind-the-scenes hockey stories, it's another packed episode of The Sheet with Jeff Marek.

#TheSheet #JeffMarek #NHL #StanleyCupFinal #VegasGoldenKnights #CarolinaHurricanes #BrianBurke #BillMcCreary #NHLPlayoffs #hockeylover

videos.title: Vår Tesla kostade 13 000 kr i månaden i värdeminskning
videos.summary_source_text:
Vår Tesla kostade 13 000 kr i månaden i värdeminskning Vi köpte en Tesla Model Y ny 2021 för 890 000 kronor och sålde den tre år senare för 420 000. Bara värdeminskningen blev ungefär 13 000 kronor i månaden. I det här klippet visar jag varför det inte är ett unikt misstag, utan ungefär vad en ny bil kostar.

Räknar man med allt (värdeminskning, alternativkostnad, service, skatt, parkering och alla småkostnader) landar de flesta nya bilar på 6 000 till 10 000 kronor i månaden. Även på en begagnad bil är det svårt att komma under 4 000 till 5 000. Vår gamla V70 var lika dyr att äga som den billiga Peugeot vi har nu.

Bilkalkylen vi använder finns för dig som är supporter på Patreon. Mer om bilekonomi finns på rikatillsammans.se och i RikaTillsammans-podden.

videos.title: BEFORE YOU PULL ANOTHER NOSE HAIR, WATCH THIS | Dr. Mandell
videos.summary_source_text:
BEFORE YOU PULL ANOTHER NOSE HAIR, WATCH THIS | Dr. Mandell Before you trim or pull another nose hair, you may want to know what it's actually doing for your health. Most people see nose hair as an annoyance, but these tiny hairs serve as your body's first line of defense against dust, pollen, pollution, bacteria, and other airborne particles. In this video, you'll discover how nose hairs, mucus, and microscopic cilia work together as a remarkable filtration and cleaning system that protects your respiratory tract with every breath you take. Learn why your nose is much more than a breathing passage and why nature put those hairs there for a very important reason. What may seem insignificant could be helping protect you every second of every day.

*********************************

videos.title: You’re Rotating Too Soon  #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
You’re Rotating Too Soon  #golfswing #golftips A lot of golfers start rotating their body too early in the downswing, which pulls the club over the top and makes the swing steep. When the arms don’t have time to drop, the path moves left and contact suffers. In this video we show how keeping your back facing the target for a little longer allows the body to shift and the club to shallow naturally. That sequence leads to better path, better strike, and better ball flight.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. A lot of golfers start rotating their body too early in the downswing, which pulls the club over the top and makes the swing steep. videos.title: Did the Denver Broncos do the right thing by giving Pat Surtain II an unprecedented raise?
videos.summary_source_text:
Did the Denver Broncos do the right thing by giving Pat Surtain II an unprecedented raise? Did the Denver Broncos make the right move by giving **Pat Surtain II** an unprecedented raise? The DNVR Broncos Crew breaks down what Pat Surtain’s new deal means for the Broncos, Sean Payton’s roster-building strategy, the Denver Broncos’ salary cap outlook, and the future of one of the NFL’s best cornerbacks.

Pat Surtain II has been the centerpiece of the Denver Broncos defense, but was this contract decision the right call for the franchise? What does it mean for Riley Moss, Ja’Quan McMillian and JAhdae Barron?

Plus, NFL Network’s Brian Baldinger joins the show to discuss.

On the DNVR Broncos Podcast, Zac Stevens, Ryan Koenigsberg and Henry Chisholm are coming to you LIVE from the DNVR Bar to break down all of the latest news in Broncos Country.

videos.title: What Direction Should The Mavericks Go In The Draft To Pair A Star With Cooper Flagg?
videos.summary_source_text:
What Direction Should The Mavericks Go In The Draft To Pair A Star With Cooper Flagg? Become a DIEHARD! alldlls.com/diehard

The Mavs’ no. 9 pick is a must-hit for Dallas- what route should the Mavericks go? Best player available or best fit? We dive into Ron Harrod Jr.’s first mock draft- which you can find here: https://alldlls.com/2026-nba-mock-draft/. Plus, Duke's Cameron Boozer is following in the footsteps of his Blue Devil brethren, Cooper Flagg, putting together a dominant freshman season. A bruising, polished forward who's reshaped how scouts talk about the 2026 NBA Draft- we break down Cam Boozer's play-style and his Duke Blue Devils dominance, and whether the Mavericks have the assets to climb that high on the 2026 NBA Draft board. Plus, NBA insider Marc Stein joins the conversation to talk Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs taking on Jalen Brunson and the New York Knicks in the Finals, and we give our predictions.

#cooperflagg #dallasmavs #nba #pjwashington #danielgafford #klaythompson #najimarshall #jasonkidd #khrismiddleton #kyrieirving #marvinbagley #derecklively 

0:00 - Intro
5:53 - Cam Boozer player evaluation- we break down his style of play, reveal where we have him on our big board and discuss his fit on potential teams
19:00 - We huff some hopium when discussing Ron's new mock draft
33:47 - Stein Line time- the chatter he's hearing around the league re: Wemby and the Spurs making it to the Finals with such a young core. Plus, further discussion on the Mavs and Stars announcing their moves to new arenas
50:02 - Previewing game one of the finals and we give our picks for who will win the series

videos.title: Jack Eichel Zone Entry Success
videos.summary_source_text:
Jack Eichel Zone Entry Success Everyone thinks Jack Eichel’s zone entries are all about speed, and it plays a role but it’s how he uses that speed that makes him so dangerous.

In this short, we break down three learnable mechanics that you can learn from Eichel to start having more successful zone entries:

1️⃣ Attack with speed - force defenders to back up before you cross the blue line, better to attack on angle.

2️⃣ Receive the puck in a crossunder - getting on an edge helps with pass reception and disguises your next move.

3️⃣As always, a wide stance for better balance, increased optionality and better puck protection mechanics. 

Master these, and you’ll start generating more zone entries. Visit our website to take your first steps to building these mechanics into your game next season.

Everyone's posting "GPT-5.6 specs REVEALED" — leaked benchmarks, a 1.5 million token context window, a locked release date. One problem: OpenAI hasn't announced ANY of it. No blog post, no model card, no official numbers. So in this video we separate the signal from the hype: what OpenAI has genuinely shipped, what the leaks are actually pointing at, and how it stacks up against Anthropic's Claude.

By the end you'll know exactly where the rumor mill is guessing, where it's extrapolating from real patterns, and what any of this means if you actually use these tools for work.

- The confirmed lineup — GPT-5, GPT-5.5, and GPT-5.5 Instant
- The hard line between official facts and Codex log leaks
- The 1.5M token context rumor (and why the direction matters more than the number)

- A full spec comparison across GPT, Claude, and earlier models
- Realistic release timing, rollout path, and the risks nobody puts in the thumbnail

videos.title: Canadiens Shock Carolina... Then Everything Changed | ECF Rewind
videos.summary_source_text:
Canadiens Shock Carolina... Then Everything Changed | ECF Rewind Cinematic rewind of the Eastern Conference Final between the Montreal Canadiens and the Carolina Hurricanes with behind-the-scenes exclusive footage. The Montreal Canadiens struck first, but the Carolina Hurricanes had the final say.

From Juraj Slafkovsky's Game 1 heroics to Frederik Andersen's shutout in Game 4 and Carolina's dominant series-clinching performance in Game 5, relive every key goal, save and turning point from the 2026 Eastern Conference Final. Carolina ultimately outshot Montreal 167-89 over the five-game series and earned its first trip to the Stanley Cup Final in two decades.

After dropping Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Final, the Hurricanes responded with four consecutive victories to capture the Prince of Wales Trophy and advance to the Stanley Cup Final for the first time since 2006. Along the way, Carolina won back-to-back overtime thrillers, received clutch performances from Nikolaj Ehlers and Andrei Svechnikov, and overwhelmed Montreal with relentless pressure throughout the series.

▶️ SUBSCRIBE: https://youtube.com/nhl

🔥MORE NHL VIDEOS: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL1NbHSfosBuGoyOevGOoJ6nHPD7nDRVFn&si=MHZlxPpXKq2JlQx1

videos.title: SO CLOSE! Carolina Hurricanes Drop Game 1 in a NINE Goal Battle
videos.summary_source_text:
SO CLOSE! Carolina Hurricanes Drop Game 1 in a NINE Goal Battle While the Carolina Hurricanes took a 2-0 lead in the first period, they ultimately lost Game 1, 5-4, to the Vegas Golden Knights. Despite the loss, the Hurricanes showed that they can beat these guys. Once they clean up the small mistakes, the Hurricanes will take some games from Vegas as the series goes on. What stood out in Game 1 to give the sense that the Hurricanes have a solid chance to even the series on Thursday night? On the Wednesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on Game 1 as a whole. The third line led the way, scoring 3 of the 4 goals in Game 1. Nikolaj Ehlers scored two goals, giving the Hurricanes an early lead, and Jordan Staal scored the third goal for the line to tie it up. Even Shayne Gostisbehere got into the scoring. Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on how there are some positives to take away from the game. Despite the loss, the Hurricanes have what it takes to win some games in the Stanley Cup Final. Lastly, Zach concludes the show by sharing his thoughts on what was said postgame from Rod Brind'Amour, Gostisbehere, and Seth Jarvis. They gave some insight into the confidence of this team and how they will bounce back. All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: 19-year-old Blades Brown's swing is INSANE!
videos.summary_source_text:
19-year-old Blades Brown's swing is INSANE! Ahead of the 2025 Butterfield Bermuda Championship, Blades Brown walked us through his swing and broke down his driver shots.

On Tuesday, June 2, the now 19-year-old pro accepted Special Temporary Membership to the PGA TOUR. In seven starts on the PGA TOUR this season, Brown recorded four top-25 finishes, highlighted by a solo third-place finish at the Puerto Rico Open. In his first start of the 2026 season, Brown finished T18 as a sponsor exemption at The American Express, where he carded a second-round 60 to earn a spot in Saturday’s final pairing alongside World No. 1 Scottie Scheffler. Brown is currently No. 11 on the Korn Ferry Tour Points List in his first season as a member.

videos.title: The Golden Knights Strike First + Analyzing the Yzerplan w/ Max Bultman | Daily Faceoff Live
videos.summary_source_text:
The Golden Knights Strike First + Analyzing the Yzerplan w/ Max Bultman | Daily Faceoff Live Welcome to Daily Faceoff Live!

The Vegas Golden Knights have struck first in the Stanley Cup Final.

After falling behind early, Vegas stormed back to take Game 1 on the road, becoming the first team to overcome a multi-goal deficit and win the series opener. We break down the comeback, key performances from Nikolaj Ehlers and Tomas Hertl, Shea Theodore's bounce-back effort, and the turning points that helped the Golden Knights take a 1-0 series lead.

We also play The % Game and debate:

The chances this ends up being the highest-scoring game of the Stanley Cup Final
Whether Mitch Marner could emerge as the Conn Smythe favorite if Vegas goes on to win the Cup

Reactions to the NHL's new All-Star Weekend format
The possibility of Russia returning for the World Cup of Hockey
Why the NHL is not currently pursuing expansion

Later, Max Bultman joins the show to discuss the Detroit Red Wings and take a closer look at the Yzerplan. Has Steve Yzerman made meaningful progress in Detroit, and what comes next for the Red Wings as they try to become a Stanley Cup contender?

videos.title: How to Choose the Right Skate Hollow
videos.summary_source_text:
How to Choose the Right Skate Hollow In this video, Chase from IW links up with J.P. at Elite Blade Performance to break down one of the biggest performance factors in skating: your blade hollow. Whether you're looking for more bite in your turns, smoother glide down the ice, or a better balance of both, this breakdown will help you understand how different hollows affect your performance and find the right setup for your skating style.

In this video, we cover:
✅ What is a hollow, and how does it affect skating
✅ What is a specific starting hollow for entry and youth players
✅ When should you increase hollow size

videos.title: Game 1 Chaos: Knights Beat Canes | Morning Cuppa Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Game 1 Chaos: Knights Beat Canes | Morning Cuppa Hockey Season 3 rolls on with another loaded edition of Morning Cuppa Hockey as Jonny Lazarus and Colby Cohen break down all the latest from around the hockey world following a chaotic Game 1 of the Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights.

The guys open the show by sharing their initial reactions from last night’s Game 1, including what stood out most after Vegas stormed back to beat Carolina 5-4 on a late Tomas Hertl winner, and whether the Golden Knights’ resilience will continue into game 2.

Then, the Empty Netters join the show to dive deeper into the matchup, discussing Carolina’s 2-0 early lead sparked by, Vegas’ ability to claw back into the game, and whether Game 1 changed how each side should be viewed moving forward in the series.

Also, Riley Armstrong joins to break down a deep crop of Philadelphia Flyer's prospects, including David Jiricek’s NHL ceiling, Porter Martone’s star potential, and what to make of intriguing defenders like Spencer Gill and Carter Amico as they transition toward the pro game.

Later, Chris Peters stops by to go through his latest NHL Entry Draft rankings, including a deeper look into the top 10 projected player in the draft and several other standout names climbing the ranks.

Don't forget to subscribe, like, and turn on notifications so you never miss an episode of Morning Cuppa Hockey.

videos.title: New Tool Tracks Data Centers In Your Neighborhood
videos.summary_source_text:
New Tool Tracks Data Centers In Your Neighborhood Is an AI data center coming to your neighborhood?

SAVE this post so you don't forget to check your zip code later…

Erin Brockovich (the same one who had a movie made about her) just launched a new crowdsourced map to help communities track exactly where AI data centers are popping up.

Whether you’re worried about the environmental impact, power grid, or just curious about what's being built down the street, this is a pretty cool and useful tool.
Just go to brockovichdatacenter.com to check it out.

If You Learn This You'll Play The Best Golf Of Your Life #shorts #golf #golfswing #ericcogorno #shorts 

In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno reveals the most overlooked fundamental in the golf swing – hips moving foward during the downswing. Eric Cogorno demonstrates how moving your hips in golf swing forward by 4-6 inches creates two essential benefits: superior low point control for ball-first contact and an inside swing path for straighter shots. Through detailed analysis of PGA Tour Pro swings and practical golf drills, you'll master this non-negotiable movement that transforms inconsistent ball strikers into solid players virtually overnight.

What You'll Learn:

• How to use your hips in the golf swing to move 4-6 inches forward for solid contact
• Essential low point control techniques that ensure ball-first, ground-second impact
• Golf drills using alignment sticks to practice proper hip movement in the golf swing
• PGA Tour Pro hip movement patterns from Scotty Scheffler, Tommy Fleetwood, and Rory McIlroy
• Two proven feelings for initiating forward hip drive during your golf swing
• Proper setup positions that make forward hip movement easier and more natural

videos.title: Signs Your Body Has Become Protective
videos.summary_source_text:
Signs Your Body Has Become Protective Sometimes the body becomes protective in ways that are easy to miss.
Bracing. Hesitation. Guarded movement. Disconnection. Difficulty trusting the body.
In this video, I explore some of the signs that your body may have become stuck in patterns of protection — and why recognizing them can be the first step toward meaningful change.
If this feels familiar and you’d like to explore this work more deeply, you’re welcome to book a one-on-one Zoom call with me here:
https://calendly.com/taroiwamoto/45min
And if you’d like to begin with direct experience, I’ve created a free guided somatic session here:
https://taro-iwamoto.thinkific.com/courses/masterclass

If you feel ready for deeper, more structured support, you can learn more about my course here:
https://taroiwamoto.com/thecourse

I help self-aware adults who are exhausted by recurring pain, tension, and fear cycles despite everything they’ve tried, and who are ready to stop chasing fixes and follow a deeper somatic, nervous-system-based path to lasting change, so they can trust their body again and fully live their life with ease, freedom, and confidence.

videos.title: Jean Alesi’s One and Only Win | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast: Legends
videos.summary_source_text:
Jean Alesi’s One and Only Win | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast: Legends At the 1995 Canadian Grand Prix, Jean Alesi achieved the sole Grand Prix victory of his Formula 1 career.

Speaking to Tom Clarkson, Jean relives the standout moments from that unforgettable moment in Montreal – like only finding out from the fans that he was leading the race, doing a lap of honour on the back of Michael Schumacher’s car, and then being whisked to the Vatican in Rome to celebrate.

He talks about the emotions he felt when crossing the finish line and what he feels now when looking back – and whether only winning one race frustrates him.

Jean also shares some brilliant stories from other times in his career – telling Tom how he helped inspire Eddie Jordan to make his own F1 team and why Ayrton Senna was somewhat bemused when Jean borrowed something from him.

videos.title: Use the lead elbow to get better distance and control…
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
Use the lead elbow to get better distance and control… Keeping the lead arm straight to long will give you all kinds of problems. Let it bend and the path of the club will become much better. #golftips #golfswing #pga 

0:00 Intro to when to bend your lead arm/elbow

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 Keeping the lead arm straight to long will give you all kinds of problems. Let it bend and the path of the club will become much better. #golftips #golfswing #pga

videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Code Worked All Night (No Human) 👑 Premium Community: https://mrc.fm/premium

Last week I showed you tank. My self hosted mission control for Claude Code that runs entirely on my own server. This week I pushed it as far as it goes. I queued up real work, walked away, and let Claude Code build on my app all night long with zero human in the loop. No babysitting, no approving every step, no me. videos.title: Avalanche Collapse, Stanley Cup Final, Broncos Minicamp & NFL Blockbuster Trades| After the Horn
videos.summary_source_text:
Avalanche Collapse, Stanley Cup Final, Broncos Minicamp & NFL Blockbuster Trades| After the Horn The Colorado Avalanche season is officially over, and Jesse Montano joins Ron Zappolo and Tom Green to break down one of the most shocking playoff exits in franchise history. What went wrong against Vegas? Did the Avs underachieve? And what does the future hold for Jared Bednar, Joe Sakic, and the Avalanche heading into a pivotal offseason?

Plus, the crew previews the Stanley Cup Final, debates whether Vegas is the favorite, discusses the NBA Finals matchup, and reacts to some of the biggest NFL news of the week — including major trades involving Myles Garrett and A.J. Brown.

The show also pays tribute to two legendary sports figures, including Colorado Avalanche icon Claude Lemieux and NFL Hall of Famer Raymond Berry.

🏒 Colorado Avalanche playoff collapse
🏒 Jared Bednar's future with the Avs
🏒 Stanley Cup Final preview: Vegas vs Carolina
🏀 NBA Finals discussion and championship predictions
🏈 Broncos minicamp updates and Bo Nix outlook
🏈 Myles Garrett traded to the Rams
🏈 A.J. Brown traded to the Patriots
⚾ Rockies season update and trade deadline discussion
💙 Remembering Claude Lemieux and Raymond Berry

videos.title: Fibromyalgia Is NOT in Your Head — Here's the REAL Cause
videos.summary_source_text:
Fibromyalgia Is NOT in Your Head — Here's the REAL Cause You’re not imagining your fibromyalgia pain or symptoms. Discover common fibromyalgia causes and triggers, and learn how to find fibromyalgia relief by addressing the underlying issue.

0:00 Introduction: Fibromyalgia explained 
0:42 Fibromyalgia causes and triggers 
1:45 Low vitamin D and fibromyalgia
3:19 Mitochondrial damage and fibromyalgia symptoms
5:00 What to do for fibromyalgia relief
5:31 Fibromyalgia research and recovery
7:58 Fixing low vitamin D 
8:39 Magnesium deficiency and fibromyalgia symptoms 
10:34 More fibromyalgia relief tips

🌟 10 biological signals control how you feel every day, but only 1 matters most for YOUR body. Take the free 2-minute quiz to find out: https://drbrg.co/4u5mRuZ

Watch next for natural strategies to reduce inflammation and improve your health:
▶️ https://youtu.be/Ixf0DFExhq0
▶️ https://youtu.be/Sn7T46fCjLE

Read more about fibromyalgia causes and solutions here: https://drbrg.co/3PSFUuG

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

Did you know that when antibodies from fibromyalgia patients were injected into healthy lab mice, those mice developed pain hypersensitivity?

Low vitamin D is very common in people with fibromyalgia. Vitamin D controls almost every part of your immune system, especially the natural killer cells. Fibromyalgia is an immune problem, so fixing a vitamin D deficiency can be one of the most powerful tools for mitigating fibromyalgia symptoms.

Fibromyalgia is also related to low magnesium, low zinc, and existing autoimmune diseases. 

If you have fibromyalgia, you must reestablish the proper fuel for your nerve cells. Ketones can bypass the damage and start feeding your neurons. Fasting has shown significant benefits for fibromyalgia relief and recovery. 

If you have fibromyalgia, prioritize rest over exercise and ensure you focus on increasing the right nutrients.

videos.title: What 67 Teaspoons of Sugar Does to Your Body
videos.summary_source_text:
What 67 Teaspoons of Sugar Does to Your Body Your blood sugar may look “normal” on a test… but that doesn’t mean your body is healthy.

🌟 Which of the 10 health signals are you missing? Take the 2-minute quiz to discover your #1 Health Lever: https://drbrg.co/48WVqfl

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements.

In this video, Dr. Berg reveals the shocking truth about how much hidden sugar the average person consumes every single day, not just from sweets, but from bread, pasta, cereal, crackers, waffles, pancakes, muffins, and other starches.

You’ll discover how insulin works like a vacuum cleaner to remove sugar from your blood, and why this process can silently damage your metabolism for 15–20 years before blood sugar problems finally appear.

Learn the early warning signs of insulin resistance, why “normal” blood sugar can be misleading, and what really happens inside your body when insulin stops working properly.

Watch until the end to understand the hidden process behind weight gain, fatigue, prediabetes, and type 2 diabetes.

videos.title: Böj den främre aren för bättre kontroll och längd…
videos.channel: Bättre & Enklare golf med Marcus Edblad PGA
videos.summary_source_text:
Böj den främre aren för bättre kontroll och längd… Håll gärna den främre armen rak men inte för länge. Böj den i tid för bättre och längre slag. #golf #golftips #golflife 

0:00 Intro till när vi ska böja främre armen. 

Nu finns det möjlighet att genom appen Skillest göra mig till din personliga tränare. Du hittar mig på denna länk: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Låt mig hjälpa dig finna bättre slag, bollträff, riktning och svinghastighet. Oavsett om du slår en wedge, järnklubba eller driver kan du med dina händer lära dig golfslag som du inte trodde var möjliga. Längre och rakare slag kräver alltid träning men något alla kan lära sig.  

Vare sig du fightas med en slice eller en hook så har jag videos som kan guida till bättre golf. Är du nybörjare så kan du börja med min grönt kort eller nybörjarkurs.

Försök ersätta dåliga vanor med nya bättre vanor. Oavsett vilket loft eller skaft din klubba har så är det du som bestämmer vad som ska hända. Oavsett om det står callaway, ping, titleist eller taylor made på klubban så kan du slå bra slag.

Var noga med vad du tittar på. Det finns många duktiga instruktörer på nätet, några av mina favoriter är Mike Malaska, Mike Dunaway, Mike Austin.

Försök fokusera på grunderna i slaget bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår. Träna med fördel på en sak i taget. Tränar du din svinghastighet måste du tillåta att det går snett och tränar du raka slag kan du inte samtidigt svinga i max fart.

Ha tålamod och låt det ta sin tid, luras inte av genvägar som bara gör att du tappar fokus från det viktiga. Golf kommer alltid vara svårt men vi kan med träning se till att vi blir bättre för varje dag som går.

Titta mycket på golf på TV. Bryson Dechambeu, Dustin Johnson, Rory Mcilroy kan inspirera till längre slag och andra som Tiger Woods, Viktor Hovland, Ludvig Åberg och Phil Mickelson kan ge dig fantasi att skapa nya slag.

Träna styrketräning för hela kroppen för att må bra genom livet och skapa en effektiv maskin. Träna händer, handleder och armar för att skapa en effektiv release och mer svinghastighet.

Du kan finna inspiration i filmer som Happy Gilmore, Tin Cup eller Bagger Vance. Filmer som Rocky kan ge dig motivation att ta dig till gymet. Legender som Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone eller Hulk Hogan kan få dig att vilja bli starkare och Moe Norman, Arnold Palmer, Ben Hogan, Sam Snead eller Jack Nicklaus kan inspirera dig att bli en bättre golfare. 

Ge aldrig upp i sökandet och träningen för att få bästa bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår.

Filmat med iPhone 17 och redigerat i iMovie på en Macbook. Håll gärna den främre armen rak men inte för länge. Böj den i tid för bättre och längre slag. #golf #golftips #golflife 

0:00 Intro till när vi ska böja främre armen. 

Nu finns det möjlighet att genom appen Skillest göra mig till din personliga tränare. Du hittar mig på denna länk: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Låt mig hjälpa dig finna bättre slag, bollträff, riktning och svinghastighet. Oavsett om du slår en wedge, järnklubba eller driver kan du med dina händer lära dig golfslag som du inte trodde var möjliga. Längre och rakare slag kräver alltid träning men något alla kan lära sig.  

Vare sig du fightas med en slice eller en hook så har jag videos som kan guida till bättre golf. Är du nybörjare så kan du börja med min grönt kort eller nybörjarkurs.

Försök ersätta dåliga vanor med nya bättre vanor. Oavsett vilket loft eller skaft din klubba har så är det du som bestämmer vad som ska hända. Oavsett om det står callaway, ping, titleist eller taylor made på klubban så kan du slå bra slag.

Var noga med vad du tittar på. Det finns många duktiga instruktörer på nätet, några av mina favoriter är Mike Malaska, Mike Dunaway, Mike Austin.

Försök fokusera på grunderna i slaget bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår. Träna med fördel på en sak i taget. Tränar du din svinghastighet måste du tillåta att det går snett och tränar du raka slag kan du inte samtidigt svinga i max fart.

Ha tålamod och låt det ta sin tid, luras inte av genvägar som bara gör att du tappar fokus från det viktiga. Golf kommer alltid vara svårt men vi kan med träning se till att vi blir bättre för varje dag som går.

Titta mycket på golf på TV. Bryson Dechambeu, Dustin Johnson, Rory Mcilroy kan inspirera till längre slag och andra som Tiger Woods, Viktor Hovland, Ludvig Åberg och Phil Mickelson kan ge dig fantasi att skapa nya slag.

Träna styrketräning för hela kroppen för att må bra genom livet och skapa en effektiv maskin. Träna händer, handleder och armar för att skapa en effektiv release och mer svinghastighet.

Du kan finna inspiration i filmer som Happy Gilmore, Tin Cup eller Bagger Vance. Filmer som Rocky kan ge dig motivation att ta dig till gymet. Legender som Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone eller Hulk Hogan kan få dig att vilja bli starkare och Moe Norman, Arnold Palmer, Ben Hogan, Sam Snead eller Jack Nicklaus kan inspirera dig att bli en bättre golfare. 

Ge aldrig upp i sökandet och träningen för att få bästa bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår.

Filmat med iPhone 17 och redigerat i iMovie på en Macbook.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

videos.title: The BEST drivers I've ever tested 😳⛳️
videos.summary_source_text:
The BEST drivers I've ever tested 😳⛳️ Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: Most powerful BMW M3 EVER v Audi R8 GT: DRAG RACE
videos.summary_source_text:
Most powerful BMW M3 EVER v Audi R8 GT: DRAG RACE It’s time for another head-to-head with the BMW M3 CS Touring going up against an Audi R8 GT! They’ll compete in a quarter-mile drag race, a half-mile rolling race, and a 100mph brake test - but which will come out on top?

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-0306

Does a high-performance wagon have what it takes to bring down one of the lightest, most recognisable supercars around? Well, to find out we’re putting the R8 GT up against a BMW M3 CS Touring! 

But just how different are these two high-performance beasts? Let’s take a closer look at the stats… 

First up we have the M3 CS Touring. Under the bonnet, it ‘only’ has a 3-litre twin-turbo straight-six, producing 550hp and 650Nm. Power is sent to all four wheels via an 8-speed automatic gearbox, and as new it costs £120,600. It’s clearly the heavier of the two cars, weighing 1,850kg.

Then we come to the R8 GT. It’s powered by a naturally aspirated 5.2-litre V10, and this creates 620hp and 565Nm. However, unlike the M3 CS, it’s exclusively Rear Wheel Drive! But on the flip side, it’s a whole lot lighter than the BMW, weighing in at just 1,570kg! 

So the BMW can send power to all four wheels, but it’s almost 300kg heavier than the R8! Will weight prevail over AWD? There’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE!

New Audi S5 v Audi RS Legends: https://bit.ly/Audi-S5-v-Rivals It’s time for another head-to-head with the BMW M3 CS Touring going up against an Audi R8 GT! They’ll compete in a quarter-mile drag race, a half-mile rolling race, and a 100mph brake test - but which will come out on top?

Then we come to the R8 GT. It’s powered by a naturally aspirated 5.2-litre V10, and this creates 620hp and 565Nm. However, unlike the M3 CS, it’s exclusively Rear Wheel Drive! But on the flip side, it’s a whole lot lighter than the BMW, weighing in at just 1,570kg! 

So the BMW can send power to all four wheels, but it’s almost 300kg heavier than the R8! Will weight prevail over AWD? There’s only one way to find out for sure… LET’S RACE!

videos.title: The Battle Shifts to Chicago, Colorado Eagles Go On the Road With the Series Tied
videos.summary_source_text:
The Battle Shifts to Chicago, Colorado Eagles Go On the Road With the Series Tied The Western Conference Final is all square, and now the Colorado Eagles hit the road for a pivotal stretch in Chicago. After making history by reaching the conference final for the first time as an AHL franchise, the Eagles continue their remarkable Calder Cup run fueled by elite defense, timely scoring, and the outstanding play of goaltender Trent Miner.

In this episode, Meghan Angley and Jesse Montano break down Colorado's dramatic comeback series win over Coachella Valley, recap Games 1 and 2 against the Wolves, and examine the key factors that have driven the Eagles' postseason success.

Plus, what can Colorado take from splitting the first two games, and what will it take to steal a pair on the road against a tough Chicago team? With a trip to the Calder Cup Final on the line, every shift matters as the Eagles and Wolves battle in a best-of-seven showdown. videos.title: 2.6: Hertl wins Game 1 with GORGEOUS goal - MULTIPLE CALLS 🇨🇿⭐
videos.summary_source_text:
2.6: Hertl wins Game 1 with GORGEOUS goal - MULTIPLE CALLS 🇨🇿⭐ Tomas Hertl certainly has a flair for the dramatic! Hertl gives his Golden Knights a 5-4 lead with 3:24 left in regulation with a sensational shot, a score that Vegas holds onto as they win Game 1 of the Stanley Cup Final against Carolina. Hertl's goal is the latest go-ahead goal in any Stanley Cup Final game since Patric Hornqvist's goal in Game 6 of the 2017 Final. Hertl's late-game heroics earn him tonight's Top European Player of the Night honors! Listen to the Czech, USA, Canada, Spanish, Finnish, French, Swedish, German, Danish and ASL broadcasts call the action as Hertl secures Game 1 for Vegas.

videos.title: UK | 02nd May, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
UK | 02nd May, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.summary_source_text:

Justin then talks about a key Colorado recruit Ryan Pankey taking a visit to campus. Finally, Justin talks about why Mike Leach likely going into the College Football Hall of Fame shows how absurd it is that Sonny Lubick is not honored among the all-time legends of the sport. videos.title: Karlsson: ”Jättekul” att spela final igen

videos.title: Hindi | 02nd May, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
Hindi | 02nd May, Tuesday - RIFT Presentation 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

RIFT is a next-generation trading interface built on top of Hyperliquid, designed to simplify and accelerate how users interact with modern financial markets.

Instead of manually buying and selling assets, RIFT allows you to take positions on price movements (up or down) across crypto and other markets — powered by ultra-fast execution and intelligent automation.

⚡ Key Features:
• Lightning-fast trade execution using advanced infrastructure
• Smart trade selection and structured opportunities
• Built-in risk management (stop-loss, take-profit, position sizing)
• Leverage options to amplify potential returns
• Clean, mobile-first interface via Telegram Mini App
• Demo mode to test strategies with zero risk

🤖 What makes RIFT different?
RIFT combines AI-driven analysis + automated execution, scanning multiple market opportunities and structuring them into simple, actionable trades — all within seconds.

You don’t need to be a professional trader or understand complex charts. RIFT does the heavy lifting, helping you participate in markets with speed, structure, and efficiency.

📊 Built for the modern era of trading, where automation and algorithmic execution dominate over manual trading.

⚠️ Disclaimer: Trading involves risk. Always understand fees, leverage, and market conditions before trading. 🚀 RIFT Protocol – The Future of Automated Trading

videos.title: Chris MacFarland Out As Avs GM, Takes Over in Nashville. Joe Sakic Back In His Old Role...For Now
videos.summary_source_text:
Chris MacFarland Out As Avs GM, Takes Over in Nashville. Joe Sakic Back In His Old Role...For Now Colorado Avalanche rocked by Chris MacFarland’s surprise exit as GM, jumping to the Nashville Predators as President of Hockey Operations and GM. How will this swift departure impact the Avs' offseason strategy—especially with Joe Sakic stepping back into the GM role and negotiations looming for a key Cale Makar extension? Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson debate whether this move stabilizes the bench for Jared Bednar or creates further uncertainty in the front office.

Key discussion points include MacFarland’s incomplete legacy in Colorado, questions about his draft pick management, and the Avalanche’s depleted prospect pool. The hosts explore potential new GM candidates, organizational culture, and Bednar’s job security in a now pivotal contract year. Plus, a reaction to Cale Makar’s second-place finish in Norris Trophy voting and a fresh season grade for Mackenzie Blackwood round out this can’t-miss Avalanche analysis.

videos.title: Better Posture = Better Turn  #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Better Posture = Better Turn  #golfswing #golftips If your posture is off at address, your body won’t be able to rotate properly in the swing. An S-posture (over-arching) or a C-posture (rounded) both restrict how your hips, ribs, and shoulders can turn. In this video, we show you how to find a neutral spine at setup so your body can rotate freely and efficiently. Fixing this one position can immediately improve your consistency and power.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. If your posture is off at address, your body won’t be able to rotate properly in the swing. From his days growing up around Pebble Beach to hosting the return of Golf Channel's Big Break, Wells shares incredible stories from golf, television, country clubs, celebrity golf rounds, and life behind the scenes of reality TV.

The Bachelor in Paradise bartender and Golf Channel personality dives into his love for golf, his favorite courses in the world, playing alongside celebrities, and why the return of Big Break could be one of the biggest moments in golf entertainment this year. videos.title: 🎙️ In Danish: Ehlers scores 25 SECONDS into Game 1! 🇩🇰
videos.summary_source_text:
🎙️ In Danish: Ehlers scores 25 SECONDS into Game 1! 🇩🇰 Nikolaj Aufeldt has the Danish call on Viaplay as "the Great Dane" Nikolaj Ehlers collects the puck at the Carolina blueline, races down the ice and fires a shot past Carter Hart to open the scoring just TWENTY-FIVE SECONDS into Game 1 of the Stanley Cup Final! The goal is the third-fastest goal from the start of a Stanley Cup Final, trailing only Toronto's Sid Smith (15 seconds in against Montreal in 1951) and Philadelphia's Reggie Leach (21 seconds in against Montreal in 1976).

videos.title: Gary Bettman Addresses NHL Expansion, All-Star Game Changes, Succession Plans & More
videos.summary_source_text:
Gary Bettman Addresses NHL Expansion, All-Star Game Changes, Succession Plans & More Jonny Lazarus, Irfaan Gaffar, and David Pagnotta break down the biggest headlines from NHL Commissioner Gary Bettman's State of the League press conference ahead of Game 1 of the Stanley Cup Final in Raleigh, North Carolina.

The trio reacts to Bettman's comments on the NHL's new All-Star Game format, which will feature a best-on-best international tournament built around young stars from Canada, the United States, Sweden, Finland, and the Rest of the World. They also discuss the league's continued revenue growth, expansion speculation, NCAA eligibility concerns, the future of player development, and what Bettman had to say about his eventual succession plan as commissioner.

Plus, the panel examines the latest surrounding the Vegas Golden Knights' decision to block Bruce Cassidy from speaking with the Edmonton Oilers and what it could mean moving forward.

From league business to the future direction of the NHL, the crew breaks down everything fans need to know from Bettman's annual media availability ahead of the Stanley Cup Final.

Chapters:
00:00 Introduction
00:18 New NHL All-Star Game Format
01:37 NHL Revenue Growth Update
02:18 Expansion Discussion
03:01 NCAA Eligibility & Development Concerns
04:01 Gary Bettman Succession Plans
04:36 Bettman's Future as Commissioner
05:16 Final Thoughts

The Dallas Stars have officially selected The Shops at Willow Bend in Plano as the future home of a massive new arena and entertainment district, marking a potentially historic shift for the franchise and the future of hockey in North Texas.

Owen Newkirk crew breaks down what the move means for the organization, the timeline for the project, and how it could reshape the fan experience for years to come. From state-of-the-art facilities and surrounding development plans to the impact on current arena discussions in Dallas, the crew explores the biggest questions surrounding the Stars' ambitious vision.

videos.title: You're Not Bad at Chipping. Your Setup Is Wrong.
videos.summary_source_text:
You're Not Bad at Chipping. Your Setup Is Wrong. A tour coach fixed my inconsistent chipping with one setup change, and it'll work for your game too.

Ralph Bauer coaches tour players, and in this episode he breaks down the stock chip shot every amateur should own. The whole thing comes down to where your center of gravity is at address, why dragging the handle wrecks your contact, and a rehearsal trick that makes clean contact repeatable.

Want to learn the Tour Read system?  Check out his app here: https://bit.ly/tourread-gsl

Follow Coach Ralph here https://www.instagram.com/ralphbauergolf/ A tour coach fixed my inconsistent chipping with one setup change, and it'll work for your game too.

videos.title: Jack Nicklaus on Scottie Scheffler & the Memorial Tournament | The Drop
videos.summary_source_text:
Jack Nicklaus on Scottie Scheffler & the Memorial Tournament | The Drop 18-time major winner Jack Nicklaus joins the show to help preview the Memorial Tournament, as the event he founded and hosts celebrates its 50th anniversary this week. The living legend addressed Scottie Scheffler’s chances at a three-peat, some of the most memorable moments in tournament history and the impact of Play Yellow, an organization he co-founded with his wife Barbara and the PGA TOUR to help raise money for pediatric patients. Later, 4-time PGA TOUR winner Chris Gotterup stops by and talks about how his season has gone and how past disappointments at the Memorial can help him learn how to succeed in his third start there this week. We’ll dive into the statistical history of Muirfield Village Golf Club to determine which hole is most impactful in determining a champion. And before we go, we relive Tiger Woods’ iconic chip-in on the 16th hole in 2012– a shot that propelled him to his fifth and final victory at Jack’s place.

00:35 Preview of the Memorial Tournament
01:32 Can Scottie Scheffler three-peat at Memorial?  
01:55 Notable three-peats in TOUR history 
03:00 Jack Nicklaus joins the show 
08:35 Aon Next 10 & Swing 5 Update
09:45 Chris Gotterup joins the show 
14:17 Most important hole at Muirfield?
16:44 Matt Every’s pick to win 
17:32 “Mulligan Moments” from 2026 season
18:49 Brandt Snedeker’s hole-in-one at Charles Schwab
19:20 Flashback to Tiger’s 2012 Win at Memorial
20:23 Outro

videos.title: Don’t Point Your Stick At The Net, Do This Instead
videos.summary_source_text:
Don’t Point Your Stick At The Net, Do This Instead The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

LIVE FITTING! Jr Golfer | FULL BAG A #tourtrucktuesday FULL BAG TOUR FITTING FOR A 15 YEAR OLD WHO’S DEVELOPING INTO A GREAT PLAYER

Happy Birthday Nico Nowrooz!! Thank you for spending your birthday at The Trott Shop.

Nico is new to the game but comes from Soccer / Football. He is growing and evolving as a PLYR. In this upload I work with Nico to get him into a set of golf clubs that will not only help him progress / develop as a golfer BUT will also keep his future evolution firmly in mind.

I arm Nico with some tools to help with his swing on the golf course, we use THE STRIKE WEDGE throughout. This device is proving important in my LIVE Fittings:

Learn more here:  https://strikewedge.com/products/strike-wedge?sca_ref=10203179.ZVsfXrWWRbcO

The club spec changes, we look at significantly different shafts and we modify his lie angles.

HUGE differences in the set up & a great fitting.

videos.title: Stop Only Saying "Skate Faster." The Missing Language of Modern Hockey
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Only Saying "Skate Faster." The Missing Language of Modern Hockey Power skating spends years on "skate faster" and "move your feet," but it almost never names the mechanics that actually separate NHL skaters from everyone else. There's a language underneath every great skater that most coaches have never been taught, and once you can name it, you can finally fix it.

This is a clip from our latest Train 2.0 film session with coach Shawn O'Donnell on the language underneath elite skating. The full breakdown, plus the drills to build it into your game, is inside Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: Stay Wider in the Takeaway #golfswing #golftips
videos.summary_source_text:
Stay Wider in the Takeaway #golfswing #golftips Many golfers fold their trail arm too early in the takeaway, which breaks sequence and limits torso rotation. This split grip drill helps you feel the right arm stay straighter for longer while keeping it more “on top” of the club. You’ll start the swing with better connection, width, and rotation — leading to more consistent contact and fewer compensations.
Follow for more practical golf tips from Me and My Golf. Many golfers fold their trail arm too early in the takeaway, which breaks sequence and limits torso rotation. videos.summary_source_text:
STOP BUYING BREAD UNTIL YOU CHECK THIS | Dr. Mandell STOP BUYING BREAD UNTIL YOU CHECK THIS. Most people assume that if a loaf says whole grain, multigrain, wheat, or healthy on the front, it must be a good choice. Unfortunately, that is not always true. In this video, you'll discover the simple things I look for before buying bread, how to identify misleading labels, what ingredients to avoid, and what really matters when it comes to fiber, blood sugar, energy, and overall health. A few seconds spent reading the package can make a big difference for your health and your family's health. Watch until the end so you know exactly what to look for the next time you're standing in the bread aisle.

videos.title: Inkomst eller sparkvot: vad ger mest för din ekonomi?
videos.summary_source_text:
Inkomst eller sparkvot: vad ger mest för din ekonomi? Det är lättare att bli rik genom att öka inkomsten än att snåla. Det stämmer till stor del. En löneökning gör mer för ekonomin än att skära i alla roliga utgifter.

Men inkomst utan sparvanor tenderar att äta upp sig självt. Livsstilsinflation är verklig. Formeln som faktiskt fungerar är att öka inkomsten och automatisera sparandet direkt på lönedagen, så att båda sidorna arbetar parallellt.

Mer om sparande och inkomststrategier finns på rikatillsammans.se och i RikaTillsammans-podden.

#shorts #privatekonomi #ekonomi #spara #lön #inkomst Det är lättare att bli rik genom att öka inkomsten än att snåla. Det stämmer till stor del. En löneökning gör mer för ekonomin än att skära i alla roliga utgifter.

Men inkomst utan sparvanor tenderar att äta upp sig självt. Livsstilsinflation är verklig. Formeln som faktiskt fungerar är att öka inkomsten och automatisera sparandet direkt på lönedagen, så att båda sidorna arbetar parallellt.

videos.summary_source_text:
Claude Mythos: The AI Model Anthropic Built But WON'T Sell You Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Anthropic finished training their most powerful model ever — and then refused to ship it. It's called Claude Mythos, and it's been sitting above the entire Opus line this whole time. Here's everything we know.

Anthropic has quietly confirmed a model that sits above Claude Opus 4.6, 4.7, and 4.8 — internally codenamed Mythos (and "Capybara"). It's not Opus 5. Anthropic describes it as a new tier entirely: larger, more intelligent, and locked behind a private cybersecurity program called Project Glasswing.
In this breakdown I cover what Claude Mythos actually is, the leaked benchmark numbers (93.9% on SWE-bench Verified vs Opus 4.6's 80.8%), how it autonomously chained exploits and rediscovered a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug, how it stacks up against GPT-5 and Gemini Ultra, what it costs ($25/$125 per million tokens), why you can't access it, and what it means for coding, knowledge work, and education even if you never touch the API.
A finished frontier model that's too dangerous to release. Either the biggest vibe shift in AI safety culture — or the most expensive marketing move in the industry. Let's get into it.

⏱️ Chapters below 👇
00:00 — The model Anthropic won't sell you
00:40 — What Claude Mythos Actually Is
01:38 — How We Got Here: The Timeline
02:43 — What Mythos Can Actually Do
06:30 — How It Stacks Up Against GPT and Gemini
08:43 — Release, Access & Why You Can't Have It
10:09 — What Mythos Means For You, Even If You Can't Use It
12:11 — The Risks: Why Anthropic Is Scared of Their Own Model


🔔 Subscribe for AI breakdowns without the hype: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCGIiO6Uo8V8vkuLOcFvf3ZQ/

videos.title: The Secret to Making Healthy Habits Stick
videos.summary_source_text:
The Secret to Making Healthy Habits Stick Most people know what they need to do to improve their health. Eat better. Exercise more. Sleep better. Spend less time on screens. The challenge is not a lack of information, it's finding the energy and discipline to make those changes stick. Silence can be the bridge between knowing what to do and actually doing it.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.


About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Cardiovascular Interventions, P.A. in Orlando Florida where, since 1990,  he has been repeatedly recognized in local publications as a Top Doctor performing thousands of interventional procedures in hospital and out patient settings. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease . He is also a clinical assistant professor of medicine at The Florida State University and University of Central Florida. Most people know what they need to do to improve their health. Eat better.
